title: Safe and Alive: A Poetry Essay on Real Love
description:
When you stop protecting someone from your life and start weaving them into it, something shifts.

This essay explores a poem about the moment you imagine someone being loved by your people—your mama, your family, your world. It's the moment when safety and aliveness stop being a choice and become one thing.

Full essay with poem: [LINKS_PLACEHOLDER]

Topics covered:
- Why safety alone isn't enough
- Why aliveness without safety is reckless
- The specific moment of real integration
- What it means to dissolve in someone's arms
- How real love demands both
